what_it_does: Defines a 'Motion & space' React canvas that lets the user view and edit four token scales — spacing, radii, shadows, motion (easings + durations) — each in its own tabbed sub-panel with live editing, per-token lock, copy-var, revert, a snapshot/compare feature, and a live preview card that uses all four scales. It also has a 'Generate with Vi' panel and per-shadow 'Refine' that call window.CV_AI.complete to propose JSON token edits the user can adopt.
observations: Hardcoded BASE_* arrays define the default scales: 13 spacing steps (8px base + 4px half), 7 radii (4→pill 999), 6 warm-tinted shadows, 5 motion tokens.
edits/setEdit/snapshot/setSnapshot/locked/toggleLock are props — the component edits a session token-overrides object owned by the parent.

generate() builds a long brand-context prompt (ivory paper #FBF7EC, ink #1F1A12, gold #E0C010, warm shadows, 8px spacing) and asks for compact JSON {intent, entries[]}, with a regex fallback to extract JSON from non-strict replies.
Locked tokens are passed to the prompt as 'keep exactly' and skipped during adopt; adoptOne/adoptAll respect the lock.
Shadow panel embeds a RefinePop that calls CV_AI to revise a single box-shadow value, again parsing {value} JSON with regex fallback.
Motion panel renders the cubic-bezier curve as an SVG path with an animateMotion play dot, plus a CSS-keyframe slide demo (dsa-pat-slide) replayed via a playKey counter.
PatternPreviewBlock applies token values as inline CSS custom properties on a local style object so the snapshot preview is isolated from :root edits.
Numeric inputs clamp values (spacing max 400, radii max 120, duration max 3000) and re-emit as px/ms strings.
Registers window.Patterns; aliases hooks as useState_pt/useMemo_pt (useMemo imported but unused).
An animateMotion dur reads editsObj['dur-base'] || '600ms' (uses the edited base duration to time the curve replay).
standouts: Three distinct CV_AI touchpoints with shared JSON-with-regex-fallback parsing: bulk generate, per-shadow RefinePop refine — all constrained to the warm paper aesthetic in-prompt.
Lock mechanism is honored both in the UI and inside the generation prompt ('LOCKED tokens to keep exactly'), so AI proposals respect user-pinned values.
Snapshot/compare: takes a frozen copy of edits and renders it side-by-side with live edits via isolated inline custom properties.
Live SVG bezier curve + animated play dot makes easing tokens directly legible and replayable.
conventions: Follows window-global registration (window.Patterns) and React-via-globals; tokenized look via CSS vars and dsa-* classes.
Routes all model calls through window.CV_AI.complete per the AI-registry directive; never calls a provider directly.
The edit-as-override pattern (edits object keyed by token name, setEdit(name, undefined) to revert) is consistent across all four panels.
Default values live in the BASE_* arrays in this file rather than being read from the token registry — a potential second home for token defaults.
concerns: BASE_SPACING/RADII/SHADOWS/MOTION duplicate token default values inline; if the real token registry diverges these defaults silently drift from the system's actual tokens.
JSON-parse fallbacks swallow parse errors silently (empty catch blocks), surfacing only a generic toast.
useMemo_pt is imported but never used.
proposal.entries keyed by array index; minor but fragile.
The shadow textarea and easing/value inputs accept free-form strings with no validation before setEdit, so an invalid CSS value could be adopted.
notes: A four-scale (spacing/radii/shadow/motion) token-tuning canvas with per-token lock, snapshot compare, a unified live preview, and three CV_AI-driven proposal flows constrained to the brand aesthetic.
questions: Are the BASE_* defaults meant to mirror the real token registry, and if so how are they kept in sync?
Where do edits/snapshot/locked actually persist, and how does 'Export to disk' turn them into a CSS patch?
Is RefinePop defined elsewhere and shared, given it is used here without definition?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header ('Spacing · radii · shadows · motion — tokens that quietly hold every component together') matches the implemented four-scale token editor with live preview and AI proposals.
apparent_themes: Tokens as the editable single source for look-and-feel
AI proposals constrained by an explicit brand aesthetic and user locks
Direct manipulation with live, isolated preview and compare
intention_signals: Aims to let the user retune the system's quiet structural tokens by direct manipulation or AI proposal, while protecting locked values and the paper-first aesthetic, and seeing the effect immediately in one representative card.
novelty: Combines token editing, per-token locking that AI must respect, a frozen snapshot/compare via isolated inline custom properties, and a live SVG bezier-curve replay in one canvas.
contribution: Contributes the motion-and-space token-tuning canvas: four scale editors, lock/snapshot/revert UX, an all-four-scales live preview, and three brand-constrained CV_AI proposal flows (bulk generate, per-shadow refine) with robust JSON-fallback parsing.
summary_for_embedding: A React 'Motion & space' canvas editing spacing/radii/shadow/motion tokens with per-token lock, snapshot compare, live preview, and CV_AI-driven JSON token proposals constrained to a warm paper aesthetic.
intention_for_embedding: To let the user retune the design system's structural tokens by hand or via AI proposals that respect locked values and the brand aesthetic, seeing the effect live in one card.
